description: Web surface attack preconditions. HTTP/HTTPS/Onion targets.

WB-01.label: web_reachable
WB-01.evidence_hint: HTTP GET root returns any status

WB-09.label: env_file_exposed
WB-09.description: .env or config file with credentials accessible

WB-10.label: default_credentials
WB-10.evidence_hint: Auth succeeds with admin:admin, vendor defaults

WB-11.label: tls_weak_or_missing
WB-11.description: TLS not enforced, weak protocol, or cert expired

WB-13.label: cve_version_match
WB-13.description: Server version matches known CVE pattern

WB-14.label: auth_surface_present
WB-14.evidence_hint: HTTP 401/403 or login form at auth paths

WB-18.label: redirect_chain_present
WB-18.evidence_hint: >2 redirects in chain to final URL

WB-21.label: webshell_present
WB-21.description: Web shell or backdoor script accessible on the server
WB-21.evidence_hint: HTTP 200 on /shell.php, /c99.php, cmd.php with command output

WB-22.label: ssti_injectable
WB-22.description: Server-side template injection detected — template expression evaluated in response
WB-22.evidence_hint: Expression like {{7*7}} returns 49, or Jinja2/Twig/Freemarker eval

WB-23.label: unauth_api_endpoint
WB-23.description: Unauthenticated API endpoint accessible without credentials

WB-24.label: robots_path_disclosure
WB-24.description: robots.txt or sitemap.xml discloses hidden paths or restricted areas
WB-24.evidence_hint: Disallow: entries in robots.txt or sitemap paths pointing to admin/internal areas

WB-32.label: debug_endpoint_accessible
WB-32.description: Debug or actuator endpoint exposing environment variables or configuration

WB-33.label: health_endpoint_exposed
WB-33.description: Health or status endpoint leaks version or internal service information

WB-34.label: metrics_exposed
WB-34.description: Prometheus or Spring metrics endpoint accessible without authentication

WB-35.label: xmlrpc_exposed
WB-35.description: XML-RPC endpoint active and responding (brute-force or SSRF risk)
WB-35.evidence_hint: HTTP 200 on /xmlrpc.php, /xmlrpc with XML method response

WB-37.label: version_disclosed_structured
WB-37.description: Version string present in structured JSON/XML endpoint response
WB-37.evidence_hint: version/ver/release key in JSON response body or XML version field

WB-38.label: credentials_in_config
WB-38.description: Sensitive key (password/secret/token/key) found in accessible config endpoint
WB-38.evidence_hint: password=, secret=, token=, api_key= patterns in /config.json or similar

WB-39.label: internal_ip_disclosed
WB-39.description: Private IP address present in structured endpoint response

WB-40.label: jsonl_event_stream_exposed
WB-40.description: JSONL event stream accessible without authentication

WB-41.label: sqli_injectable
WB-41.description: SQL injection vulnerability confirmed in web application parameter or form field
WB-41.evidence_hint: SQL error pattern, boolean-based, or time-based injection response differential

WB-42.label: xss_injectable
WB-42.description: Cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability confirmed — user input reflected unescaped
WB-42.evidence_hint: Script tag or canary string reflected verbatim in response body

WB-43.label: cmdi_injectable
WB-43.description: OS command injection confirmed — shell command output present in response or timing delay detected
WB-43.evidence_hint: uid= in response body, or response time delta >3s on sleep payload

web_sqli_to_shell_v1.label: Web SQLi to Shell
web_sqli_to_shell_v1.description: SQL injection confirmed — escalate to OS command execution via INTO OUTFILE or UDF

web_full_chain_v1.label: Web Full Chain (SQLi)
web_full_chain_v1.description: Alias for web_sqli_to_shell_v1 — full exploitation chain via SQL injection

web_cmdi_to_shell_v1.label: Web CMDi to Shell
web_cmdi_to_shell_v1.description: Command injection confirmed — execute reverse shell payload

web_ssti_to_rce_v1.label: Web SSTI to RCE
web_ssti_to_rce_v1.description: Server-side template injection confirmed — craft template payload for remote code execution

web_default_creds_to_admin_v1.label: Web Default Credentials to Admin
web_default_creds_to_admin_v1.description: Default credentials accepted — gain admin access and deploy webshell

web_struct_fetch_v1.label: Web Structured Endpoint Analysis
web_struct_fetch_v1.description: Structured endpoint exposure — config/API/debug endpoints accessible without auth

web_surface_v1.label: Web Surface Enumeration
web_surface_v1.description: Web target reachable and enumerable — sufficient surface for attack planning
